title: Use remote state with locking
description: Store state in a shared remote backend with state locking so concurrent runs cannot corrupt it, never local files on a team project.
body: Use when more than one person or pipeline runs Terraform against the same resources. Do not keep state in a local file or commit tfstate to git, and do not disable locking to 'unstick' a run. Actions: configure a remote backend (S3+DynamoDB, GCS, Terraform Cloud) with locking enabled, verify the lock is acquired at plan/apply start, and release it cleanly on exit. Validation signals include a lock entry appearing during a run and clearing afterward, and no two runs proceeding at once. Risk boundary: a force-unlock can let two applies race and corrupt state; only force-unlock after confirming no other run holds it, and back up state first.
